#include "wrappers.h"

#define JELATINE_UTIL_H   (1)
#define dbg_printf(...)   dbg_printf_internal(__FILE__, __LINE__, __VA_ARGS__)
#define dbg_error(...)   dbg_error_internal(__FILE__, __LINE__, __VA_ARGS__)
#define dbg_unreachable()   dbg_unreachable_internal(__FILE__, __LINE__)
#define dbg_warning(...)   dbg_warning_internal(__FILE__, __LINE__, __VA_ARGS__)
#define dbg_abort()   abort()
#define load_int16_un(src)   load_int16_un_internal((const char *) (src))
#define load_uint16_un(src)   load_uint16_un_internal((const char *) (src))
#define store_int16_un(dst, val)   store_int16_un_internal((char *) (dst), (val))
#define load_int32_un(src)   load_int32_un_internal((const char *) (src))
#define store_int32_un(dst, val)   store_int32_un_internal((char *) (dst), (val))
#define c_try
#define c_catch(exc)
#define c_throw(exc,...)   c_throw_dbg(__FILE__, __LINE__, exc, __VA_ARGS__)
#define c_rethrow(exc)   longjmp(*(thread_self()->c_exception.buf), (exc))

Define Documentation

#define c_catch (   exc  ) 
Value:
thread_self()->c_exception.buf = __prev__; \
}                                              \
(exc) = thread_self()->c_exception.data; \
if (exc == 0) {                          \
} else

'catch' clause of a C pseudo-exception block. The variable exception must be an integer which will hold the caught exception

Parameters:
exc An integer holding the exception if caught

#define c_try
Value:
{                                                       \
    int __exc__;                                        \
    jmp_buf __buf__;                                    \
    jmp_buf *__prev__ = thread_self()->c_exception.buf; \
    thread_self()->c_exception.buf = &__buf__;          \
    thread_self()->c_exception.data = 0;                \
    __exc__ = setjmp(__buf__);                          \
    if (__exc__ != 0) {                                 \
        thread_self()->c_exception.data = __exc__;      \
    } else

'try' clause of a C pseudo-exception block. Pseudo-exceptions can be thrown inside this block by using c_throw()

Replacement function for strchrnul() which is a GNU extension

filename The name of the source file in which this function was called
lineno The line where this function was called

Parameters:
filename The name of the file from which the function was called
lineno The number of the line in which the function was called
format A format string using the printf() guidelines

int  lineno,
const char *  format,

filename The name of the source file in which this function was called

format A format string using the printf() guidelines
